steps.title: Identify where the y-intercept occurs
steps.content: The y-intercept of a function is the point where its graph crosses the y-axis. This always happens at x = 0.

steps.title: Read the y-value from the graph
steps.content: Find x = 0 on the horizontal axis and trace vertically upward to where the exponential curve intersects the y-axis. The intersection occurs at y = 3. The answer is 3.
